revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Inbe-jinja]] [[en:Inbe Shrine]] [[ja:忌部神社]] [[simple:Inbe Shrine]] {{Expand Japanese|忌部神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Inbe Shrine (Q11490722) | native_name = 忌部神社 | image = Inbe Shinto shrine.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Inbe Shrine (Q11490722) | coordinates = {{coord|34.060278|134.545556}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Amenohiwashi|lt=Amenohiwashi|ja|天日鷲神|ko|아메노히와시노카미|zh|天日鷲神|qid=Q10939697|1=Otori-sama}} }} {{nihongo|'''Inbe- Shrine'''|忌部神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Beppyo shrine|lt=Beppyo Shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|mr|बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|simple|Beppyo shrine|zh|別表神社|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q9610964|ltq=Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings|lt=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings|qid=Q135009132|1=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings}} * {{ill|Q135022918|ltq=Q135022918|qid=Q135022918|1=Shikinai Successor}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)|ja|阿波国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11657514|1=List of Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q207309|ltq=Q207309|qid=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.060278|134.545556}} == image (P18) == * Inbe Shinto shrine.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q10939697|ltq=Q10939697|qid=Q10939697|1=Otori-sama}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20060723061802/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/540301.html == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917280|ltq=Q134917280|qid=Q134917280|1=Kokuhei Chūsha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Japan|lt=Japan|ab|Иапониа|ace|Jeupang|ady|Японие|af|Japan|als|Japan|alt|Јопон|ami|Ripon|am|ጃፓን|ang|Iapan|anp|जापान|an|Chapón|arc|ܝܦܢ|ar|اليابان|arnews|تصنيف:اليابان|arquote|اليابان|ary|الجاپون|arz|اليابان|ast|Xapón|as|জাপান|atj|Japan|avk|Nipona|av|Япония|awa|जापान|ay|Nihun|azb|ژاپون|az|Yaponiya|azquote|Yaponiya|ban|Jepang|bar|Japan|bat_smg|Japuonėjė|ba|Япония|bbc|Japang|bcl|Hapon|bdr|Gipun|be_x_old|Японія|be|Японія|bew|Jepang|bg|Япония|bgnews|Категория:Япония|bh|जापान|bi|Japan|bjn|Japang|blk|ဂျပန်ခမ်းထီ|bm|Japan|bn|জাপান|bnquote|জাপান|bnvoyage|জাপান|bo|ཉི་ཧོང་།|bpy|জাপান|br|Japan|bs|Japan|bsnews|Kategorija:Japan|btm|Jepang|bug|ᨍᨛᨄ|bxr|Япон Улас|ca|Japó|canews|Categoria:Japó|caquote|Japó|cbk_zam|Japón|cdo|Nĭk-buōng|ceb|Hapon|ce|Ипнойчоь|chr|ᏣᏩᏂᏏ|ch|Chapan|chy|Japan|ckb|ژاپۆن|co|Giappone|crh|Japon|csb|Japòńskô|cs|Japonsko|csnews|Kategorie:Japonsko|csquote|Japonsko|csvoyage|Japonsko|cu|Ꙗпѡнїꙗ|cv|Япони|cy|Japan|dag|Japan|da|Japan|de|Japan|denews|Kategorie:Japan|dequote|Japan|devoyage|Japan|din|Japan|diq|Japonya|dsb|Japańska|dtp|Jepun|dty|जापान|dv|ޖަޕާނު|dz|ཇ་པཱན|ee|Japan|el|Ιαπωνία|elnews|Κατηγορία:Ιαπωνία|elquote|Ιαπωνία|elvoyage|Ιαπωνία|eml|Giapòṅ|en|Japan|ennews|Category:Japan|enquote|Japan|envoyage|Japan|eo|Japanio|eonews|Kategorio:Japanujo|eoquote|Japanio|eovoyage|Japanio|es|Japón|esnews|Categoría:Japón|esquote|Japón|esvoyage|Japón|et|Jaapan|etquote|Jaapan|eu|Japonia|ext|Japón|fa|ژاپن|fanews|رده:ژاپن|faquote|ژاپن|favoyage|ژاپن|ff|Japan|fiu_vro|Jaapan|fi|Japani|finews|Luokka:Japani|fivoyage|Japani|fj|Japani|fo|Japan|frp|Japon|frr|Jaapan|fr|Japon|frnews|Catégorie:Japon|frquote|Japon|frvoyage|Japon|fur|Gjapon|fy|Japan|gag|Yaponiya|gan|日本|ga|An ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いんべじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 146655738 == Library of Congress authority ID (P244) == * n85315903 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8480005000907 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00631926 == Commons category (P373) == * Inbe-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Imuheno Shrine|lt=Imuheno Shrine|qid=Q135041321|1=Imuheno Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '徳島県徳島市二軒屋町2丁目48', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '徳島県吉野川市山川町忌部山', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Inbe-jinja}} [[EN:Inbe Shrine]] [[JA:忌部神社]] [[SIMPLE:Inbe Shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q11490722}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|'''Inbe Shrine in Tokushima City'''|other Inbe shrines}} {{神社 |名称 = Inbe Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Inbe Shinto shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|qid=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|qid=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}} Nikenya-chō 2-48 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|03|37|N|134|32|44|E|scale:30000_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Kami|Q10939697|qid=Q10939697|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=Otori-sama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=myōjin great shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）successor shrine
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}25 ({{ill|lt=1892|Q7839|qid=Q7839|1=1892}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-sheet roofing |例祭 ={{ill|lt=October 19|Q2961|qid=Q2961|1=October 19}} |地図={{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Inbe Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|03|37|N|134|32|44|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|03|37|N|134|32|44|E}}}} }} '''Inbe Shrine''' (いんべじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nikenya-chō|Q11371805|qid=Q11371805|1=Nikenya-chō}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima|Q207309|qid=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|qid=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is treated as the successor shrine of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kokuhei chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is now a {{ill|lt=Beppyo shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Jinja Honchō|Q908077|qid=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Kami|Q10939697|qid=Q10939697|lt=Ame-no-Hiwashi-no-Mikoto|1=Otori-sama}} (あめのひわしのみこと) An ancestral deity of the Awa {{ill|lt=Inbe clan|Q8189229|qid=Q8189229|1=Inbe clan}}, and according to ''{{ill|lt=Kogo Shūi|Q531555|qid=Q531555|1=Kogo Shūi}}'', at the time of Amaterasu’s hiding in the rock cave, he followed {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, planted {{ill|lt=paper mulberry|Q389185|qid=Q389185|lt=kaji trees|1=Paper mulberry}}, and made white cloth offerings woven from {{ill|lt=kōzo|Q4097283|qid=Q4097283|1=Broussonetia kazinoki × B. papyrifera}} fibers (also called {{ill|lt=yū cloth|Q11519320|qid=Q11519320|1=yū cloth}}). === History === The “Inbe Shrine of Oe District, Awa Province” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} had also been a myōjin taisha, but after the medieval period its location became unknown due to warfare and other causes. Since multiple shrines claimed to be the shikinaisha Inbe Shrine in early modern times, in {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|qid=Q7746|1=1871}} (Meiji 4) it was provisionally ranked kokuhei chūsha with “location unknown,” and in the next year the village shrine {{ill|lt=Inbe Shrine (Yoshinogawa)|Q11490723|qid=Q11490723|lt=Inbe Shrine|1=Inbe Shrine (Yoshinogawa)}} in Yamazaki village, Oe District (now Yamakawa-chō, {{ill|lt=Yoshinogawa|Q592500|qid=Q592500|1=Yoshinogawa, Tokushima}}), was designated as the shikinaisha Inbe Shrine. In response, Goshō Shrine at Nishibatayama in {{ill|lt=Mima District|Q1156496|qid=Q1156496|1=Mima District, Tokushima}} (now Sadamitsu, {{ill|lt=Tsurugi|Q1206005|qid=Q1206005|1=Tsurugi, Tokushima}}; now this shrine’s external {{ill|lt=sessha|Q135009904|qid=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} {{ill|lt=Goshō Shrine (Tsurugi)|Q119929720|qid=Q119929720|lt=Goshō Shrine|1=Gosho Shrine}}) asserted that claim. Although in {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|qid=Q7761|1=1874}} another {{ill|lt=Daijōkan proclamation|Q11444008|qid=Q11444008|1=Dajōkan Edict}} reconfirmed the Yamakawa identification, controversy continued. In {{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|qid=Q7808|1=1881}} (Meiji 14), it was changed to Goshō Shrine, but then Yamazaki side strongly objected. As a compromise, the {{ill|lt=Daijōkan (Meiji period)|Q125768223|qid=Q125768223|lt=Daijōkan|1=Dajōkan}} ordered establishment of a new shrine site at Tomiura-chō, {{ill|lt=Myōdō District|Q1156486|qid=Q1156486|1=Myōdō District, Tokushima}} (now Tokushima City). In {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|qid=Q7821|1=1885}} (Meiji 18), the current location on the mid-slope of {{ill|lt=Bizan (Tokushima)|Q8189109|qid=Q8189109|lt=Mt. Bizan|1=Mount Bizan}} was selected; it was temporarily relocated into {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11646489|qid=Q11646489|lt=Kotohira Shrine|1=Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)}} and Goshō Shrine was made an external sessha. With completion of the shrine buildings, it was moved to the present site on May 15, {{ill|lt=1892|Q7839|qid=Q7839|1=1892}} (Meiji 25). For the history of the shikinaisha Inbe Shrine, see {{ill|lt=Inbe Shrine (Yoshinogawa)|Q11490723|qid=Q11490723|1=Inbe Shrine (Yoshinogawa)}}. === Rituals === * Spring grand festival “Washi-gae” ritual (May 15) - held to commemorate the relocation (founding at the current site) in Meiji 25. === Shrine buildings === As noted above, the buildings were established in Meiji 25. In {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|qid=Q5240|1=1945}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 20), most major buildings were lost in war damage. The present honden was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|qid=Q18585|1=1953}} (Shōwa 28): front three bays, two bays on each of the other three sides, {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof and ridge ornaments (chigi and katsuogi). A loom is placed in the weaving hall. === Sessha: Goshō Shrine === *See {{ill|lt=Goshō Shrine (Tsurugi)|Q119929720|qid=Q119929720|lt=Goshō Shrine|1=Gosho Shrine}}. === Aratae cloth === According to the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, Awa {{ill|lt=Inbe clan|Q8189229|qid=Q8189229|lt=Inbe|1=Inbe clan}} were to present ''aratae'' (rough hemp cloth woven as divine garments) for the imperial {{ill|lt=Daijōsai|Q11433653|qid=Q11433653|1=Daijosai}} enthronement rite. Their descendants are said to be the Miki family. At the 1990 Daijōsai, hemp grown in Tokushima was woven at {{ill|lt=Inbe Shrine (Yoshinogawa)|Q11490723|qid=Q11490723|lt=Inbe Shrine|1=Inbe Shrine (Yoshinogawa)}} in Yoshinogawa, and rituals were conducted with the Tokushima-city Inbe Shrine chief priest as celebrant{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=Weekly Nihon no Jinja No.67 Inbe Shrine / Ōasahiko Shrine|publisher=DeAgostini Japan|date=2015-5-28|page=}}. Torii and bamboo fences were set up in hemp fields; ceremonies included seeding, harvest, first spinning, and first weaving. The selected unmarried weavers wove the cloth, and the finished aratae was placed in a kara-hitsu chest and sent to the Imperial Palace. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shikinaisha Research Society (ed.), ''Survey Report on Shikinaisha'', Vol. 23 Nankaidō, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kogakkan University|1=UNKNOWN}} Press, 1987. {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Inbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Inbe Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Inbe Shrine]][[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima, Tokushima]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:眉山]] [[Category:Inbe clan]]
